codeReview.summary: The Heading component tests have been successfully enhanced from 3 to 13 comprehensive tests. All expected behaviors are covered: title rendering, description rendering, and optional description handling. Additional coverage includes semantic HTML (h2 element), CSS class verification, accessibility testing, and edge cases.

issues.description: The handoff reports 14 tests, but the actual test file contains 13 tests. This is a minor reporting discrepancy.

sharedStateObservations.observation: No gaps found. The worker followed test conventions from AGENTS.md: using @testing-library/react, descriptive test names, and group tests with describe blocks.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: AGENTS.md specifies 'Use @testing-library/react' and 'Use descriptive test names' - both followed

sharedStateObservations.observation: The feature's skillName is 'worker', but no specific skill file exists in .factory/skills/ for worker procedures. The worker followed a reasonable procedure: read existing tests, understand component, enhance tests, verify with npm run test:run.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: AGENTS.md provides testing guidance including test structure and running tests

sharedStateObservations.observation: No additional services were needed. The worker used standard npm commands already documented in AGENTS.md (npm run test:run, npm run types, npm run check).
sharedStateObservations.evidence: AGENTS.md contains Testing & Validation Guidance with exact commands

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker discovered and fixed a test bug: initial test for empty string used queryByText('') which matched multiple elements. Fixed by using queryByRole('paragraph'). This pattern could be documented for future workers.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript shows test initially failed, then was fixed with queryByRole

summary: Feature component-tests-heading successfully implemented with 13 comprehensive tests covering all expected behaviors (title rendering, description rendering, optional description) plus additional coverage for semantic HTML, CSS classes, accessibility, and edge cases. All tests pass. Minor issue: handoff reports 14 tests but actual is 13.
